description: 1940 bomber aircraft family
description_wiki: The North American B-25 Mitchell is an American medium bomber that was introduced in 1941 and named in honor of Brigadier General William "Billy" Mitchell, a pioneer of U.S. military aviation.

description: United States Air Force base in Tampa, Florida, USA
description_wiki: MacDill Air Force Base (MacDill AFB) (IATA: MCF, ICAO: KMCF, FAA LID: MCF) is an active United States Air Force installation located 4 miles (6.4 km) south-southwest of downtown Tampa, Florida.

description: African-themed animal theme park in Tampa, Florida
description_wiki: Busch Gardens Tampa Bay is a 335-acre (136 ha) animal theme park located in Tampa, Florida, United States, with the entire park landscaped and designed around themes of Africa.

description: country in South Asia
description_wiki: India, officially the Republic of India, is a country in South Asia.

description: city in and the county seat of Saline County, Kansas, United States
description_wiki: Salina  is a city in and the county seat of Saline County, Kansas, United States.

description: 1942 bomber aircraft family; heaviest bomber widely used by US forces in World War II
description_wiki: The Boeing B-29 Superfortress is a retired American four-engined propeller-driven heavy bomber, designed by Boeing and flown primarily by the United States during World War II and the Korean War.

description: 1942 bomber aircraft family; heaviest bomber widely used by US forces in World War II
description_wiki: The Boeing B-29 Superfortress is a retired American four-engined propeller-driven heavy bomber, designed by Boeing and flown primarily by the United States during World War II and the Korean War.

description: largest island of the Northern Mariana Islands, United States of America
island country in East Asia
description_wiki: Japan is an island country in East Asia.

description: unincorporated territory of the United States of America
description_wiki: Guam (  GWAHM; Chamorro: Guåhan [ˈɡʷɑhɑn]) is an island that is an organized, unincorporated territory of the United States in the Micronesia subregion of the western Pacific Ocean.

description: American general and politician (1906–1990)
description_wiki: Curtis Emerson LeMay (November 15, 1906 – October 1, 1990) was a US Air Force general who was a key American military commander during World War II and the Cold War.

description: American U.S. Air Force major general (1903-1988)
description_wiki: Haywood Shepherd Hansell Jr.

description: World War II installation on Guam
description_wiki: Harmon Air Force Base is a former World War II United States Army Air Forces airfield, and postwar United States Air Force Base on Guam in the Mariana Islands.

description: United States Air Force base in Guam
description_wiki: Andersen Air Force Base (Andersen AFB, AAFB) (IATA: UAM, ICAO: PGUA, FAA LID: UAM) is a United States Air Force base located primarily within the village of Yigo in the United States territory of Guam.

description: maritime branch of the United States military
description_wiki: The United States Navy (USN) is the maritime service branch of the United States Armed Forces and is designated as the navy of the United States in the Constitution.

description: bridge in Vermont to Chesterfield, New Hampshire
continent located mainly in the southwestern quadrant of the Earth
description_wiki: South America is a continent entirely in the Western Hemisphere and mostly in the Southern Hemisphere, with a considerably smaller portion in the Northern Hemisphere.

description: 1937 executive aircraft family by Beechcraft
description_wiki: The Beechcraft Model 18 (or "Twin Beech", as it is also known) is a 6- to 11-seat, twin-engined, low-wing, tailwheel light aircraft manufactured by the Beech Aircraft Corporation of Wichita, Kansas.

description: capital and largest city of Japan
description_wiki: Tokyo, officially the Tokyo Metropolis, is the capital and most populous city of Japan.

description: designated city in Osaka Prefecture, Japan
description_wiki: Osaka (Japanese: 大阪市, Hepburn: Ōsaka-shi; pronounced [oː.sa.kaꜜ.ɕi]; commonly just 大阪, Ōsaka [oː.sa.ka] ) is a designated city in the Kansai region of Honshu in Japan.

description: capital city of Aichi prefecture, Japan
description_wiki: Nagoya (名古屋市, Nagoya-shi; [naꜜ.ɡo.ja, -ŋo.ja, na.ɡo.jaꜜ.ɕi, -ŋo.jaꜜ.ɕi] , locally [na.ɡo.ja, -ŋo.ja]) is the capital and most populous city of Aichi Prefecture in Japan.

description: designated city in Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan
description_wiki: Yokohama (Japanese: 横浜; pronounced [jokohama] ) is the second-largest city in Japan by population as well as by area, and the country's most populous municipality.

description: capital city of Hyogo Prefecture, Japan
description_wiki: Kobe ( KOH-bay; Japanese: 神戸, romanized: Kōbe, pronounced [koꜜː.be] ), officially Kobe City (神戸市, Kōbe-shi; [koː.beꜜ.ɕi]), is the capital city of Hyōgo Prefecture, Japan.

description: Pacific island, part of the Northern Mariana Islands, United States
Boeing B-29 bomber which dropped the atomic bomb on Hiroshima
description_wiki: The Enola Gay  () is a Boeing B-29 Superfortress bomber, named after Enola Gay Tibbets, the mother of the pilot, Colonel Paul Tibbets.

description: bombardment of Hiroshima in Japan carried out by the United States using an atomic bomb at the end of the Second World War on 6 August 1945
